summary: This is part 2 of a Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals (BZA) meeting. The board returned from executive session and voted to deny the appeal in case BZA 2022-005. They then considered variance request BZA 2022-006 for the same property, zoned R1, seeking relief from code requirements to construct a home behind two existing structures, and voted to approve that variance. The board also discussed upcoming BZA applications, vacancies, and the next meeting date.
decisions: Motion to return from executive session – approved unanimously
Motion to deny appeal BZA 2022-005 – approved unanimously
Motion to approve variance BZA 2022-006 as submitted – approved unanimously
action_items: Zoning staff to work backwards on notification timelines for the next BZA case to ensure 10-day notice and newspaper publication requirements are met
Update township website to advertise vacancies for BZA members and zoning commission members
notable_topics: Variance request BZA 2022-006 for construction of a home behind existing structures on R1-zoned property
BZA and Zoning Commission member vacancies
Next BZA meeting scheduling (tentatively April 18 at 6:30 PM)
notes: Transcript quality is poor with many repeated filler lines (e.g., 'Board of Trustees Meeting, Clermont County, Ohio') likely due to automated transcription errors. This is a BZA (Board of Zoning Appeals) meeting, not a regular Board of Trustees meeting. The auditor's classification of the property was noted as not determinative of zoning.
title: BZA 3.21.22 2 of 2.mp4
